findings.issue: Task 9 references `ModelEntry.isDownloaded` throughout (pre-navigation check, `checkModelAvailable()`, `ChatRowUiModel.isDownloaded`). The actual `ModelEntry` class at `core-runtime/src/main/kotlin/app/sanctum/machina/core/registry/ModelEntry.kt` has exactly three fields: `model: Model`, `downloadStatus: ModelDownloadStatus`, `initStatus: ModelInitStatus`. There is no `isDownloaded: Boolean` field. The concept must be derived via `downloadStatus.status == ModelDownloadStatusType.SUCCEEDED`.
findings.fix: Replace all `ModelEntry.isDownloaded` references in Task 9 with `entry.downloadStatus.status == ModelDownloadStatusType.SUCCEEDED`. Update `ChatRowUiModel.isDownloaded` computation accordingly. Note that this field is also planned to be added to `ModelEntry` by tech-spec — if Task 3 or another wave-1 task adds it, this must be explicitly coordinated. Currently no such task claims ownership of adding `isDownloaded` to `ModelEntry`.

findings.issue: Task 10 references `ModelEntry.isDownloaded` in the Draft picker edge case description: 'In dropdown показываем только `ModelEntry` где `isDownloaded == true` (not `ModelInitStatus.Ready`)'. Same hallucinated field as Task 9 finding — `ModelEntry` has no `isDownloaded` field in the actual codebase.
findings.fix: Replace `isDownloaded == true` with `entry.downloadStatus.status == ModelDownloadStatusType.SUCCEEDED` in the Draft model picker filter logic.

findings.issue: Task 11 `depends_on: [7, 8]` is missing Task 2 as a required dependency. Task 11 requires: (a) `Model.modelId: String` field (added by Task 2 — currently `Model.kt` has no `modelId` field; `AllowedModel.toModel()` does not pass `modelId` to `Model`); (b) `AppSettingsRepository.observeDefaultModelId()` and `AppSettingsRepository.setDefaultModelId()` (added by Task 2 — current `AppSettingsRepository` interface only has `observePerModelSettings`, `savePerModelSettings`, `resetPerModelSettings`). Without Task 2, Task 11 cannot compile.

Task 9 `depends_on: [7, 8]` is missing Task 4 as an explicit dependency. Task 9 requires `ChatRepository` with methods `observeChats()`, `deleteChat()`, `updateChatTitle()` — all created by Task 4. While wave ordering (Task 4 is wave 2, Task 7/8 are wave 4) implicitly ensures Task 4 precedes Task 9, the `depends_on` header does not declare this. An agent executing Task 9 in isolation (e.g., after cherry-picking) would have no `ChatRepository.kt` to read.

Task 10 `depends_on: [7, 8]` does not declare Task 6 as a dependency, yet Task 10 directly uses `AppCorruptionState` (created by Task 6 — confirmed not present in codebase: `app/src/main/kotlin/app/sanctum/machina/engine/AppCorruptionState.kt` does not exist). Task 7 does not depend on Task 6 either, so the transitive chain does not cover this. An agent reading Task 10 would find `AppCorruptionState.kt` missing without clear guidance.
findings.fix: Add `6` to `depends_on` in Task 10 frontmatter: `depends_on: [6, 7, 8]`. Alternatively, note in Details that `AppCorruptionState.kt` is created by Task 6 (wave 3) and will exist before wave 5 starts.

findings.issue: `WarmupCoordinator.isWarmupInProgress: StateFlow<Boolean>` is referenced in the TDD Anchor test `topAppBarState_warmupInFlight_returnsLoading` and the `topAppBarState` derivation logic (`combine(..., warmupCoordinator.isWarmupInProgress)`). Task 10 itself acknowledges: 'if this field doesn't exist — implement in Task 5 or add in this task.' `WarmupCoordinator` is created by Task 5 (wave 2); the current codebase has no `WarmupCoordinator.kt`. Whether `isWarmupInProgress` will be present depends on what Task 5 implements. If Task 5 does not add it, Task 10 must add it — but Task 5 is wave 2 and Task 10 is wave 5, so coordination is needed.
findings.fix: Explicitly add `isWarmupInProgress: StateFlow<Boolean>` to the Task 5 description (or via a findings fix on Task 5's review). In Task 10's Details section, state definitively: 'Task 5 must expose `isWarmupInProgress: StateFlow<Boolean>` — verify before implementing TopAppBar state machine.'

findings.issue: Task 9 Details section states `DrawerViewModel` method signature: `fun deleteChat(chatId: Long, currentOpenChatId: Long?): Boolean` (returns `shouldPopBack`). But then the TDD Anchor and Acceptance Criteria say this returns `shouldPopBack: Boolean` synchronously from a ViewModel method that also calls `chatRepository.deleteChat(chatId)` (a suspend function). Returning a Boolean synchronously from a `viewModelScope.launch` block is not possible — the suspend call happens asynchronously. The method as designed cannot both `launch { chatRepository.deleteChat(...) }` and return `Boolean` in the same call.
findings.fix: Change `deleteChat()` to emit a `shouldPopBack` event via `SharedFlow<Long>` (emitting the deleted chatId when deletion completes) rather than returning Boolean directly. The composable observes this flow and calls `navController.popBackStack()` when the event matches `currentChatId`. Or make `deleteChat` a suspend fun called from a `LaunchedEffect` in the composable — but the VM return value approach is architecturally incorrect.

findings.issue: Task 10 says to 'remove `modelName: String` from parameters of `ChatScreen`' but `ChatScreen` is currently called from `SanctumApp.kt` with `ChatScreen(modelName = modelName, onBack = ...)`. Task 8 is supposed to rework ChatViewModel and ChatScreen — but Task 10 also modifies ChatScreen and ChatViewModel. The boundary between Task 8 (ChatViewModel rework, `ChatIdentity` introduction) and Task 10 (TopAppBar state machine, adds `TopAppBarState` to VM) creates a conflict: Task 10 step 1 says 'Добавить `TopAppBarState` sealed class в `ChatViewModel`' and step 2 says 'Добавить `warmupCoordinator: WarmupCoordinator` в `ChatViewModel`' — but Task 8 already substantially rewrites `ChatViewModel`. If Task 8 is not yet complete (which it isn't — `ChatViewModel.kt` has no `ChatIdentity`), Task 10's steps assume a partially-completed Task 8 state that may conflict with what Task 8 actually produces.
findings.fix: The task ordering is correct (Task 10 depends on Task 8), but the task description should explicitly state: 'Read the ChatViewModel produced by Task 8 before making any changes — the class structure will differ significantly from the current codebase.' Add a note that `TopAppBarState` should be added to the SAME file as `ChatIdentity` (in the Task-8-rewritten ChatViewModel).

findings.issue: Implementation hints for the corruption banner (step 7 in Details) provide nearly complete implementation: full variable name (`var showCorruptionBanner by remember { mutableStateOf(appCorruptionState.corruptionOccurred) }`), component choices (`AnimatedVisibility`, `Card`), SAF filename format with exact code snippet (`SimpleDateFormat("yyyyMMdd-HHmm", Locale.ROOT).format(Date())`). The SAF filename hint is a full code expression, not a pointer to a pattern.
findings.fix: Replace the SAF filename hint with a reference to the existing pattern in `ModelManagerScreen.kt` (line 53: `private const val TIMESTAMP_PATTERN = "yyyyMMdd-HHmm"`). Remove the inline code snippets for the `remember` block and `AnimatedVisibility` wrapper — these prescribe the solution rather than pointing to a pattern.

findings.issue: TDD Anchor file is `ChatTopAppBarStateTest.kt` but the test class tests `topAppBarState` derived from `ChatViewModel`. After Task 8 rewrites `ChatViewModel`, existing tests in `ChatViewModelTest.kt` (also created by Task 8) and new `ChatTopAppBarStateTest.kt` may test overlapping ViewModel behavior. There is no mention of whether `ChatTopAppBarStateTest` should extend or be co-located with `ChatViewModelTest`.
findings.fix: Clarify in the TDD Anchor section that `ChatTopAppBarStateTest` is a separate test file from `ChatViewModelTest` (created by Task 8). The tests are complementary: `ChatViewModelTest` covers Room/persistence flows, `ChatTopAppBarStateTest` covers UI-state derivation. Both live in `app/src/test/kotlin/app/sanctum/machina/ui/chat/`.

findings.issue: Task 11 references `appSettings.observeDefaultModelId()` as the method to derive `defaultModelId: StateFlow<String>` in `ModelManagerViewModel`. This method does not exist in the current `AppSettingsRepository` interface (which only has per-model-settings methods). The task correctly gates this on Task 2 via wave ordering, but the method name is assumed without confirmation. Tech-spec architecture section lists `getDefaultModelId()` and `setDefaultModelId()` as the method names, not `observeDefaultModelId()` (which returns a Flow). A discrepancy: tech-spec says `getDefaultModelId()` (suspend, returns once) but Task 11 needs a Flow for StateFlow derivation.
findings.fix: Verify in Task 2's implementation that `AppSettingsRepository` exposes `fun observeDefaultModelId(): Flow<String>` (not just `suspend fun getDefaultModelId(): String`). If Task 2 only adds a suspend getter, Task 11 would need `DataStore.data.map { it.defaultModelId }` directly or Task 2 must be updated to add a Flow accessor. Coordinate with Task 2 owner.

findings.issue: Task 9 uses `SwipeToDismissBox` from Material 3. The task description specifies API `androidx.compose.material3.SwipeToDismissBox`. This API was added in Material 3 1.2.0. The current `app/build.gradle.kts` dependency version should be verified. No version check was done here, but if the project uses an earlier Material 3 version, `SwipeToDismissBox` may not be available.
findings.fix: Verify `androidx.compose.material3` version in `gradle/libs.versions.toml` is >= 1.2.0 before using `SwipeToDismissBox`. If earlier, use `SwipeToDismiss` with `rememberDismissState` (the deprecated predecessor API).
